role_title: Technical Program Manager (Computer Vision), Public Sector

location.office_time_required: up to 4 days per week onsite with customers in the DMV area and/or minimum 25% travel

secondary_archetype: Public Sector Delivery / Computer Vision TPM

level_signals: 3+ years of work experience in stakeholder management or customer-facing enterprise delivery
Comp range $166,400–$249,000 base in Washington DC, consistent with mid-senior IC
Owns or supports a customer account plan independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years of work experience succeeding in stakeholder management or a customer-facing role delivering enterprise-scale applications / solutions
A deep understanding of ML operations process
A track record of structured, analytics-driven problem solving
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Willingness to be onsite with customers up to 4 days per week in the DMV area and / or prepared to travel a minimum of 25%

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: A technical background (education or professional experience with computer science, economics, statistics, engineering)
A proven track record in B2B client-facing roles and expanding client relationships
Prior experience owning the technical implementation of solutions to the government

scope.surface: Computer vision workflows and AI/ML systems for national security / U.S. government customers
scope.business_model: B2B / B2G enterprise, public sector AI delivery
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of computer vision solutions
core_responsibilities: Manage customer relationships from the executive to the end user
Work alongside customers to scope computer vision use cases that Scale's engineering team will build and you will ultimately refine
Lead or support a cross-functional project team to deliver on and exceed the customer's AI/ML objectives
Lead with a 'whatever-it-takes' mentality, proactively identifying customer needs and operator pain points to ensure customer success
Learn technical concepts and tools to necessary depth, leveraging that expertise to create solutions from scratch or drastically improve existing solutions
Partner with engineering, operations, and other public sector teams to build and deliver AI systems tailored to unique government use cases in the computer vision domain

compensation.equity_form: equity grant subject to Board of Director approval

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($166,400–$249,000 base in Washington DC)
Equity mentioned as part of compensation package
Clear product surface and customer type (U.S. government / national security computer vision)
Comprehensive benefits package described including health, dental, vision, retirement, L&D stipend, and generous PTO
red_flags: Hard requirement for active TS/SCI clearance eliminates most candidates without prior government/cleared work
Up to 4 days per week onsite in DMV area is effectively full-time onsite with minimal flexibility
90-day waiting period policy before reconsidering candidates for same role limits re-application options
No visa sponsorship implied given TS/SCI clearance requirement (cleared roles typically require U.S. citizenship)

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years requirement)

ambiguities: The 'nice to haves' section is introduced as 'many have' rather than standard 'preferred' or 'nice to have' language — treated as soft/nice-to-have given the separate explicit 'Must haves' section that follows
Travel requirement states 'up to 4 days per week onsite in DMV area and/or minimum 25% travel' — unclear if these are additive or alternatives; both captured verbatim
